ddls:
CREATE TABLE "articles" (
  "article_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each article */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the source of the article */,
  "date_published" TEXT /* Date and time the article was published */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the article */,
  "text" TEXT /* Full text of the article */,
  "compound_score" REAL /* Compound sentiment score of the article */,
  "negative_score" REAL /* Negative sentiment score of the article */,
  "neutral_score" REAL /* Neutral sentiment score of the article */,
  "positive_score" REAL /* Positive sentiment score of the article */,
  "author" TEXT /* Name of the author of the article */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL where the article can be accessed */,
  "word_count" INTEGER /* Number of words in the article */,
  "read_time" INTEGER /* Estimated time to read the article in minutes */,
  "relevance_score" REAL /* A score indicating the relevance of the article to the cryptocurrency market */,
  "image_url" TEXT /* URL of the main image associated with the article */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the article */,
  "is_breaking" INTEGER /* Indicates if the article is breaking news */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("article_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_articles_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the source (e.g., The Verge, Reuters) */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL of the source */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the source */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the news source */,
  "founded_year" INTEGER /* Year the source was founded */,
  "reputation_score" REAL /* A score indicating the reputation of the source */,
  "is_reliable" INTEGER /* Indicates if the source is considered reliable */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "topics" (
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each topic */,
  "topic_name" TEXT /* Name of the topic (e.g., Cryptocurrency Regulation, Hacking) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the topic */,
  "parent_topic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent topic to support hierarchical topic categorization */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the topic was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the topic was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("topic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "article_topics" (
  "article_topic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each article-topic mapping */,
  "article_id" INTEGER /* ID of the article */,
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the topic */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("article_topic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_article_topics_article_id FOREIGN KEY ("article_id") REFERENCES articles ("article_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_article_topics_topic_id FOREIGN KEY ("topic_id") REFERENCES topics ("topic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, admin, client) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "article_id" INTEGER /* ID of the article being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the article */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the article was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the article */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user accessing the article */,
  "is_mobile" INTEGER /* Indicates if the access was from a mobile device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_article_id FOREIGN KEY ("article_id") REFERENCES articles ("article_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "custom_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each custom report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_query" TEXT /* SQL query used to generate the report */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date the report was created */,
  "last_run" TEXT /* Date and time when the report was last run */,
  "last_result" TEXT /* Result of the last report run */,
  "is_public" INTEGER /* Indicates if the report is public or private */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_custom_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dashboards" (
  "dashboard_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dashboard */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the dashboard */,
  "dashboard_name" TEXT /* Name of the dashboard */,
  "dashboard_config" TEXT /* Configuration settings for the dashboard */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date the dashboard was created */,
  "last_viewed" TEXT /* Date and time when the dashboard was last viewed */,
  "is_public" INTEGER /* Indicates if the dashboard is public or private */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dashboard */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dashboard_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dashboards_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, admin, client) */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the permission was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the permission was last updated */,
  "expires_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the permission expires */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
